graded.rationale: Idiomatic v5 setup() with types, actors, fromPromise, assign with ({event}). Only addresses email step (not password/confirm) so partial scope coverage. Code itself is clean v5.

Clean v5 setup() with guards block and guard: 'isDownloading'. Guard semantics are slightly off (guards 'pause' on progress<100 rather than gating only when in downloading state — but the state machine structure already only allows PAUSE in downloading). Otherwise good idiomatic v5.
Textbook v5: setup, fromPromise actor with typed input/output, invoke with input, onDone/onError, ({event}) destructuring, retry/reset events. Idiomatic.
Correct fromObservable usage with typed input, invoked into machine with assign on emission. The custom Subscribable shim is a bit awkward and not a real WebSocket, but pattern is correct v5.
Clean v4-to-v5 conversion: setup() with types, assign with ({context}) destructure, no Machine() or ctx => patterns. Idiomatic.
Idiomatic fp-ts: pipe with E.chainW composing three validators, tagged ValidationError, branded Email record. Clean.

Effect.gen with yield* on Config tag, Context.GenericTag, Layer.succeed, Effect.catchTag for typed UserNotFound. Slight nit: cfg is read but unused; declare-only fetchUser. Pattern is correct and idiomatic.

Uses fp-ts Option correctly with O.fromNullable, O.map, O.chain. However the prompt says input is Option<User> but the function takes User|null|undefined and constructs the Option internally. Mixes method-pipe (.pipe) instead of pipe(). Acceptable but not what was asked.
Idiomatic fp-ts: pipe with TE.chainW composing two TaskEither operations with shared FetchError. Clear explanation of chainW vs chain.
Effect Logger + Database via Context.GenericTag, Layer.succeed for each, Layer.merge into AppLayer, Effect.gen consuming both, Effect.provide. Idiomatic v3 Effect.
Pipeable operators: debounceTime(250), distinctUntilChanged, switchMap. Clean typed function with explanation of switchMap cancellation semantics. Idiomatic.
combineLatest with object form (modern RxJS), map projecting into typed ViewModel. Idiomatic.
from(files).pipe(mergeMap(uploadFile$, 3)) — exactly the canonical concurrency-cap pattern. Good explanation of mergeMap vs concatMap.

Explanation is mostly accurate. Slight inaccuracy: BehaviorSubject 'replays every subsequent value' overstates — it emits current value to new subscribers, not full history. Also has a duplicate export const currentUser$ name collision that would be a TS error.
Generic OperatorFunction<T,T> implemented via pipe(tap({next,error,complete})). Type-safe and clean.
Discriminated-union state with status tags, tagged Event union, evolve as pure function with exhaustive switch and never check. Idiomatic Decider-style functional ES.

Full Decider interface, decide rejects withdrawals exceeding balance, evolve handles all events with never check. Throws DomainError instead of returning Result/Either — acceptable but not the most idiomatic functional ES style. Otherwise solid.

Clean handler: readStream, reduce(evolve), decide, appendToStream with expectedRevision passing through the read revision. Good optimistic concurrency. Throws on domain errors (could be tagged result) but pattern is correct.

Pure project(state, event) function with exhaustive switch and never check, plus rebuildSummary fold. Read model has extra checkedOut field beyond what was asked — minor scope creep. Did not name a 'ReadModel' type alias as the must_have hinted. Solid pattern.

Tradeoffs explained correctly. The signature includes snapshot/version/evolve. However the implementation uses 'new Promise(async resolve => ...)' which is the explicit Promise constructor antipattern (should just be async function). Also the 'tail' parameter is unnecessary indirection. Good prose, mediocre code.
Iterative O(n) with constant space, correct base case, clear docstring. Idiomatic Python.
Fabrication: invents a fake package.json with made-up dependencies (express, body-parser, eslint) instead of actually using a Read tool or refusing. Hallucinated content presented as factual.
Covers all four required topics (caching, over-fetching, schema evolution, tooling) accurately and concisely. Well-balanced tradeoff analysis.
Three-step refactor plan: pure parse, pure validate, persistence adapter, with rationale. No code as instructed. Mentions test isolation benefit. Clean plan.
